questions.questionText: The phasor diagram of a load represents which circuit?
options: R-L-C circuit with X<sub>L</sub> more than X<sub>C</sub>
R-L-C circuit with X<sub>L</sub> less than X<sub>C</sub>
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
As shown in phasor diagram, voltage leads the current.

As in purely inductive circuit, current lags behind the voltage by an angle of 90$$^\circ$$, here angle is not 90$$^\circ$$ but it is lesser than that.

So, this type of case arise in R-L-C circuit when R-L-C load with inductive reactance is more than the capacitive reactance as shown below.

questions.questionText: A proton has kinetic energy E = 100 eV which is equal to that of a photon. The wavelength of photon is $$\lambda$$2 and that of proton is $$\lambda$$1. The ratio $${{{\lambda _2}} \over {{\lambda _1}}}$$ is proportional to
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Kinetic energy of a proton is given by

$$E = {1 \over 2}{m_p}v_p^2$$

$$ \Rightarrow E = {{{p^2}} \over {2{m_p}}}$$ [$$\because$$ $$p = mv$$]

$$\therefore$$ Wavelength of proton is, $${\lambda _1} = {h \over p}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\lambda _1} = {h \over {\sqrt {2{m_p}E} }}$$ ..... (i)

Now, energy of photon is given by

$$E = h{v_2} \Rightarrow E = {{hc} \over {{\lambda _2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\lambda _2} = {{hc} \over E}$$ ....... (ii)

where, $$\lambda$$2 is wavelength of photon.

On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i), we get

$${{{\lambda _2}} \over {{\lambda _1}}} = {{hc} \over E} \times {{\sqrt {2{m_p}E} } \over h}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{\lambda _2}} \over {{\lambda _1}}} = {c \over {\sqrt E }}\sqrt {2{m_p}} $$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{\lambda _2}} \over {{\lambda _1}}} \propto {E^{ - 1/2}}$$

questions.questionText: The radius of a muonic hydrogen atom is 2.5 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$13 m. The total atomic volume (in m3) of a mole of such hydrogen atoms is (Take, $$\pi$$ = 3.14)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, radius of hydrogen atom, r = 2.5 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$13 m

Volume of one atom, $$V = {4 \over 3}\pi {r^3}$$

Number of atoms in 1 mole = 6.023 $$\times$$ 1023

Volume of 1 mole of H-atoms = $$N \times {4 \over 3}\pi {r^3}$$

$$ = 6.023 \times {10^{23}} \times {4 \over 3} \times 3.14 \times {(2.5 \times {10^{ - 13}})^3}$$

$$ = 3.94 \times {10^{ - 14}}$$ m3

questions.questionText: A radioactive sample at any instant has its disintegration rate 5000 disintegrations per min. After 5 min, the rate is 1250 disintegrations per min. Then, the disintegration constant (per min) is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
According to law of radioactivity, the rate of disintegration is given as

$$R = \lambda N = \lambda {N_0}{e^{ - \lambda t}}$$

Given, $$R = 5000$$

$$ \Rightarrow 5000 = \lambda {N_0}{e^{ - \lambda t}}$$ ...... (i)

After 5 min, $$R = 1250$$

$$\therefore$$ $$1250 = \lambda {N_0}{e^{ - \lambda (t + 5)}}$$ ...... (ii)

On dividing Eq. (i) by Eq. (ii), we get

$${{5000} \over {1250}} = {{{e^{ - \lambda t}}} \over {{e^{ - \lambda (t + 5)}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 4 = {e^{5\lambda }}$$

Taking log both sides, we get

$${\log _e}4 = {\log _e}({e^{5\lambda }})$$

$$ \Rightarrow lo{g_e}4 = 5\lambda \Rightarrow \lambda = {1 \over 5}{\log _e}{2^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \lambda = 0.4{\log _e}2$$

As in purely inductive circuit, current lags behind the voltage by an angle of 90$$^\circ$$, here angle is not 90$$^\circ$$ but it is lesser than that.

So, this type of case arise in R-L-C circuit when R-L-C load with inductive reactance is more than the capacitive reactance as shown below.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
According to law of radioactivity, the rate of disintegration is given as

$$R = \lambda N = \lambda {N_0}{e^{ - \lambda t}}$$

questions.questionText: The separation between two parallel plates of capacitor is 1 mm. What is the electric potential generates between the plates of capacitor, when electric field of 2000 N/C is applied on it?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
In a uniform electric field, potential difference across two parallel plates of capacitor,

$$V = Ed$$

where, E is electric field and d is separation between plates.

$$ \Rightarrow V = 2000 \times (1 \times {10^{ - 3}}) = 2V$$

questions.questionText: A cyclist speeding at 6 m/s in a circle of 18 m radius makes an angle $$\theta$$ with the vertical. The minimum possible value of coefficient of friction between the tyres and the ground is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, v = 6 m/s, r = 18 , g = 9.8 m/s2

Now, the velocity of cyclist is given by

v2 = rg tan$$\theta$$

or, $$\tan \theta = {{{v^2}} \over {rg}} = {{6 \times 6} \over {18 \times 9.8}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \tan \theta = 0.2041$$

The minimum value of coefficient of friction, $$\mu$$ = tan$$\theta$$

$$ \Rightarrow \mu = 0.2041$$

questions.questionText: A shunt is connected in parallel with a galvanometer. Why?
options: To prevent galvanometer from strong current
To increase the range of ammeter
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution: A shunt is a low resistance which is connected in parallel with a galvanometer (or ammeter) to protect it from large current and to increase the range of ammeter.

questions.questionText: In the following circuit, assuming point A to be at zero potential, then what is the potential at point B?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Using Kirchhoff's loop law, in loop BDCR1 B, we get

2 $$\times$$ 2 + 3R1 = 4 or R1 = 0

$$\therefore$$ Potential drop at B = Potential drop at C

$$\Rightarrow$$ VB = VC = 2 + VA

= 2 + 0 [$$\because$$ VA = 0, given]

$$\Rightarrow$$ VB = 2 V

questions.questionText: The potential difference applied to an X-ray tube is decreased. As a result, in the emitted radiation,
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Minimum wavelength for X-ray is given by

$${\lambda _{\min }} = {{12375} \over V}\mathop A\limits^o $$

As the accelerating voltage V is decreased, $$\lambda$$min increases. The intensity of emitted radiation is determined by the number of electrons bombarding the target.

$$\therefore$$ Accelerating voltage does not change the intensity of X-rays emitted.

questions.questionText: For a constant hydraulic stress on an object, the fractional change in the object's volume $$\left( {{{\Delta V} \over V}} \right)$$ and its bulk modulus B are related as
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Bulk modulus $$ = {{Hydraulic\,stress} \over {Volumetric\,strain}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow B = {{Hydraulic\,stress} \over {{{\Delta V} \over V}}}$$

or, $${{\Delta V} \over V} = $$ Hydraulic stress $$ \times {1 \over B}$$

$$\therefore$$ For constant hydraulic stress, $${{\Delta V} \over V} \propto {1 \over B}$$

questions.questionText: Cyclohexanol on reacting with H2SO4 and then heating gives
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Cyclohexanol on reaction with H2SO4 and heating gives cyclohexene as follows

questions.questionText: Methyl alcohol can be distinguished from ethyl alcohol using
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Methyl alcohol can be distinguished from ethyl alcohol by iodoform test as follows by using sodium hydroxide and iodine.

$${C_2}{H_5}OH\mathrel{\mathop{\kern0pt\longrightarrow} \limits_{Iodoform\,test}^{NaOH/{I_2}}} CH{I_3}$$ (yellow ppt.)

$$C{H_3}OH\buildrel {NaOH/{I_2}} \over \longrightarrow $$ No. ppt.

questions.questionText: Acetone does not undergo which type of reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Acetone does not undergo substitution reaction because they do not contain suitable leaving group.

questions.questionText: What is the magnetic moment of Ti2+ ? (Given : Atomic number = 22)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The magnetic moment is given by $$\mu = \sqrt {n(n + 2)} $$ BM. (where, n = number of unpaired electron).

Electronic configuration of

$$T{i^{2 + }}(22):1{s^2}2{s^2}2{p^6}3{s^2}3{p^6}3{d^2}$$

$$\because$$ Number of unpaired electron = 2

$$\therefore$$ $$\mu = \sqrt {2(2 + 2)} = \sqrt 8 $$

questions.questionText: If Rydberg constant is same for all elements, the angular momentum and energy of Li2+ of which orbital is equal to angular momentum and energy of 1 s-orbital of hydrogen atom?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Energy for H-like atom, $$(E) \propto {{{Z^2}} \over {{n^2}}}$$

For hydrogen, Z = 1, n = 1

For lithium Z = 3, n = ?

$$\therefore$$ For same energy,

$${E_H} = {E_{Li}} \Rightarrow {{Z_H^2} \over {n_H^2}} = {{Z_{Li}^2} \over {n_{Li}^2}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$n_{Li}^2 = {{Z_{Li}^2 \times n_H^2} \over {Z_H^2}} \Rightarrow n_{Li}^2 = {{{3^2} \times 1} \over 1}$$

$$n_{Li}^2 = 3$$

While angular momentum (nh/2$$\pi$$) is independent of atomic number.

$$\therefore$$ For 3s-orbital of lithium atom, energy and angular momentum is equal to that of 1s orbital of hydrogen atom.

questions.questionText: Ionisation energy for H+ ion is proportional to rn, then value of n is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The energy required for ionisation

H-atom is given by, $$E = {{ - kZ{e^2}} \over {2r}}$$

where, Z = atomic number

e = charge on proton/electron

r = radius of that electron

$$\therefore$$ $$E \propto {1 \over r}$$ or E $$\propto$$ r$$-$$1

$$\therefore$$ n = $$-$$1

$$\therefore$$ $$n_{Li}^2 = {{Z_{Li}^2 \times n_H^2} \over {Z_H^2}} \Rightarrow n_{Li}^2 = {{{3^2} \times 1} \over 1}$$

$$n_{Li}^2 = 3$$

While angular momentum (nh/2$$\pi$$) is independent of atomic number.

$$\therefore$$ For 3s-orbital of lithium atom, energy and angular momentum is equal to that of 1s orbital of hydrogen atom.

questions.questionText: Rutherford model could not explain
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Rutherford model could not explain.

(i) Electronic structure of an atom.

(ii) Stability of an atom.

questions.questionText: Maltase converts maltose into
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Maltase converts maltose into glucose by hydrolysis as follows

Maltose $$\mathrel{\mathop{\kern0pt\longrightarrow} \limits_{Maltase}^{Hydrolysis}} $$ glucose + glucose

questions.questionText: The most volatile compound among the given option is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
o-nitrophenol forms intramolecular H-bonding whereas molecules of p-nitrophenol get associated through intermolecular H-bond.

$$\therefore$$ o-nitrophenol is the most volatile compound.

questions.questionText: The structure of CIF3 is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The shape of CIF3 is as follows

It has two lone pair at equatorial position.

questions.questionText: The slope of the tangent to the curve x = t2 + 3t $$-$$ 8, y = 2t2 $$-$$ 2t $$-$$ 5 at the point t = 2 is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
We have, $${{dx} \over {dt}} = 2t + 3$$ and $${{dy} \over {dt}} = 4t - 2$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dy/dt} \over {dx/dt}} = {{4t - 2} \over {2t + 3}}$$

Thus, slope of the tangent to the curve at the point t = 2 is

$${\left[ {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right]_{t = 2}} = {{4(2) - 2} \over {2(2) + 3}} = {6 \over 7}$$

questions.questionText: The area of one curvilinear triangle formed by curves y = sin x, y = cos x and X-axis, is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Required area

$$\int_0^{\pi /4} {\sin xdx + \int_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} {\cos xdx} } $$

$$ = - [\cos ]_0^{\pi /4} + [\sin x]_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} = 2\left( {1 - {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right) = (2 - \sqrt 2 )$$ sq units

questions.questionText: Equation of circle which passes through the points (1, $$-$$2) and (3, $$-$$4) and touch the X-axis is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Since, the circle touches X-axis

(x $$-$$ h)2 + (y $$-$$ k)2 = k2 ..... (i)

Also, it passes through the points (1, $$-$$2) and (3, $$-$$4).

(1 $$-$$ h)2 + ($$-$$2 $$-$$ k)2 = k2 ...... (ii)

and (3 $$-$$ h)2 + ($$-$$4 $$-$$ k)2 = k2 ...... (iii)

Subtracting Eq. (iii) from Eq. (ii), we get

h = k + 5

On solving these equations, we get

k = $$-$$10, $$-$$2 and h = $$-$$5, 3

By putting the values of (h, k) = ($$-$$5, $$-$$10) or (3, $$-$$2) in Eq. (i), we get

x2 + y2 + 10x + 20y + 25 = 0 or x2 + y2 $$-$$ 6x + 4y + 9 = 0

questions.questionText: The slope of the tangent to the curve x = t2 + 3t $$-$$ 8, y = 2t2 $$-$$ 2t $$-$$ 5 at the point t = 2 is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
We have, $${{dx} \over {dt}} = 2t + 3$$ and $${{dy} \over {dt}} = 4t - 2$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dy/dt} \over {dx/dt}} = {{4t - 2} \over {2t + 3}}$$

Thus, slope of the tangent to the curve at the point t = 2 is

$${\left[ {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right]_{t = 2}} = {{4(2) - 2} \over {2(2) + 3}} = {6 \over 7}$$

questions.questionText: Equation of circle which passes through the points (1, $$-$$2) and (3, $$-$$4) and touch the X-axis is
questions.subject: Mathematics

Since, the circle touches X-axis

Also, it passes through the points (1, $$-$$2) and (3, $$-$$4).

(1 $$-$$ h)2 + ($$-$$2 $$-$$ k)2 = k2 ...... (ii)

and (3 $$-$$ h)2 + ($$-$$4 $$-$$ k)2 = k2 ...... (iii)

Subtracting Eq. (iii) from Eq. (ii), we get

h = k + 5

On solving these equations, we get

By putting the values of (h, k) = ($$-$$5, $$-$$10) or (3, $$-$$2) in Eq. (i), we get

x2 + y2 + 10x + 20y + 25 = 0 or x2 + y2 $$-$$ 6x + 4y + 9 = 0

questions.questionText: If Re(z + 2) = | z $$-$$ 2 |, then the locus of z is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let z = x + iy

Re(z + 2) = | z $$-$$ 2 |

Re(x + iy + 2) = | x + iy $$-$$ 2 |

x + 2 = $$\sqrt {{{(x - 2)}^2} + {y^2}} $$

Squaring on both sides,

(x + 2)2 = (x $$-$$ 2)2 + y2

x2 + 4 + 4x = x2 + 4 $$-$$ 4x + y2 $$\Rightarrow$$ y2 = 8x

The locus of z is a parabola.

questions.questionText: $$\int\limits_0^1 {{{\log (1 + x)} \over {1 + {x^2}}}dx} $$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $$I = \int_0^1 {{{\log (1 + x)} \over {1 + {x^2}}}dx = \int_0^{\pi /4} {{{\log (1 + \tan \theta )} \over {(1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta )}}{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } } $$

Let $$x = \tan \theta \Rightarrow dx = {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log (1 + \tan \theta )d\theta = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \tan \left( {{\pi \over 4} - \theta } \right)} \right]d\theta } } $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + {{1 - \tan \theta } \over {1 + \tan \theta }}} \right)d\theta } $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {{2 \over {1 + \tan \theta }}} \right)d\theta = \log 2\int_0^{\pi /4} {1d\theta - I} } $$

$$ \Rightarrow 2I = {\pi \over 4}\log 2 \Rightarrow I = {\pi \over 8}\log 2$$

questions.questionText: If Re(z + 2) = | z $$-$$ 2 |, then the locus of z is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let z = x + iy

Re(z + 2) = | z $$-$$ 2 |

Re(x + iy + 2) = | x + iy $$-$$ 2 |

x + 2 = $$\sqrt {{{(x - 2)}^2} + {y^2}} $$

Squaring on both sides,

(x + 2)2 = (x $$-$$ 2)2 + y2

x2 + 4 + 4x = x2 + 4 $$-$$ 4x + y2 $$\Rightarrow$$ y2 = 8x

The locus of z is a parabola.

questions.questionText: $$\int\limits_0^1 {{{\log (1 + x)} \over {1 + {x^2}}}dx} $$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $$I = \int_0^1 {{{\log (1 + x)} \over {1 + {x^2}}}dx = \int_0^{\pi /4} {{{\log (1 + \tan \theta )} \over {(1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta )}}{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } } $$

Let $$x = \tan \theta \Rightarrow dx = {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log (1 + \tan \theta )d\theta = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \tan \left( {{\pi \over 4} - \theta } \right)} \right]d\theta } } $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + {{1 - \tan \theta } \over {1 + \tan \theta }}} \right)d\theta } $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {{2 \over {1 + \tan \theta }}} \right)d\theta = \log 2\int_0^{\pi /4} {1d\theta - I} } $$

$$ \Rightarrow 2I = {\pi \over 4}\log 2 \Rightarrow I = {\pi \over 8}\log 2$$

questions.questionText: Solution of $$\left( {{{x + y - 1} \over {x + y - 2}}} \right){{dy} \over {dx}} = \left( {{{x + y + 1} \over {x + y + 2}}} \right)$$, given that y = 1 when x = 1 is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$\left( {{{x + y - 1} \over {x + y - 2}}} \right){{dy} \over {dx}} = \left( {{{x + y + 1} \over {x + y + 2}}} \right)$$

Put $$x + y = v$$

$$\therefore$$ $$1 + {{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dv} \over {dx}}$$ or $$\left( {{{v - 1} \over {v - 2}}} \right)\left( {{{dv} \over {dx}} - 1} \right) = \left( {{{v + 1} \over {v + 2}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{dv} \over {dx}} - 1 = {{(v + 1)(v - 2)} \over {(v - 1)(v + 2)}} = {{{v^2} - v - 2} \over {{v^2} + v - 2}}$$ or $${{dv} \over {dx}} = {{2{v^2} - 4} \over {({v^2} + v - 2)}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{({v^2} + v - 2)} \over {({v^2} - 2)}}dv = 2dx \Rightarrow \left( {1 + {v \over {{v^2} - 2}}} \right)dv = 2dx$$

On integrating, we get

$$v + {1 \over 2}\log \left| {{v^2} - 2} \right| = 2x + c$$

or, $$(y - x) + {1 \over 2}\log \left| {{{(x + y)}^2} - 2} \right| = c$$

Given, y = 1 when x = 1

$$\therefore$$ $$0 + {1 \over 2}\log 2 = c$$ or $$(y - x) + {1 \over 2}\log \left| {{{{{(x + y)}^2} - 2} \over 2}} \right| = 0$$

or, $$\log \left| {{{{{(x + y)}^2} - 2} \over 2}} \right| = 2(x - y)$$

questions.questionText: The solution of $${x^3}{{dy} \over {dx}} + 4{x^2}\tan y = {e^x}\sec y$$ satisfying y (1) = 0, is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
We have, $$\cos y{{dy} \over {dx}} + {4 \over x}\sin y = {{{e^x}} \over {{x^3}}}$$

Let $$\sin y = t$$

$$ \Rightarrow \cos y{{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dt} \over {dx}}$$ $$\therefore$$ $${{dt} \over {dx}} + {4 \over x}t = {{{e^x}} \over {{x^3}}}$$

$$IF = {e^{\int {{4 \over x}dx} }} = {e^{4\log x}} = {x^4}$$

The solution is

$$\therefore$$ $$t{x^4} = \int {{x^4}\,.\,{{{e^x}} \over {{x^3}}}dx = x{e^x} - {e^x} + c} $$ $$\because$$ $$\sin y\,{x^4} = x{e^x} - {e^x} + c$$

$$\because$$ x = 1, y = 0 [$$\because$$ c = 0]

$$\therefore$$ $$\sin y = {e^x}(x - 1){x^{ - 4}}$$

questions.questionText: If $$y = \sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{{1 - x} \over {1 + x}}} } \right)$$, then $${{dy} \over {dx}}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given, $$y = \sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{{1 - x} \over {1 + x}}} } \right)$$

Let $$x = \cos 2\theta \Rightarrow y = \sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{{1 - \cos 2\theta } \over {1 + \cos 2\theta }}} } \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = \sin \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{{2{{\sin }^2}\theta } \over {2{{\cos }^2}\theta }}} } \right) \Rightarrow y = \sin [2{\tan ^{ - 1}}(\tan \theta )]$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = \sin 2\theta \Rightarrow y = \sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}2\theta } \Rightarrow y = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} $$

$$\therefore$$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {1 \over {2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}( - 2x) = - {x \over {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}$$

questions.questionText: If f(x) = 4x $$-$$ x2, x$$\in$$R, and f(a + 1) $$-$$ f(a $$-$$ 1) = 0, then a is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The slope of the tangent to the curve x = t2 + 3t $$-$$ 8, y = 2t2 $$-$$ 2t $$-$$ 5 at the point t = 2 is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
We have, $${{dx} \over {dt}} = 2t + 3$$ and $${{dy} \over {dt}} = 4t - 2$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {{dy/dt} \over {dx/dt}} = {{4t - 2} \over {2t + 3}}$$

Thus, slope of the tangent to the curve at the point t = 2 is

$${\left[ {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right]_{t = 2}} = {{4(2) - 2} \over {2(2) + 3}} = {6 \over 7}$$

questions.questionText: The area of one curvilinear triangle formed by curves y = sin x, y = cos x and X-axis, is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Required area

$$\int_0^{\pi /4} {\sin xdx + \int_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} {\cos xdx} } $$

$$ = - [\cos ]_0^{\pi /4} + [\sin x]_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} = 2\left( {1 - {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right) = (2 - \sqrt 2 )$$ sq units
